channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israeli troops battle Hezbollah in Lebanon as fears of a wider war mount after Iran's missile attack
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli troops battled Hezbollah militants inside Lebanon on Wednesday after launching what they say are limited ground operations there.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man sentenced to 6 months in prison for effort to create giant sheep hybrids
item.description: A Montana man was sentenced to six months in prison for crimes committed during his effort to create a giant sheep hybrid for captive hunting, the Department of Justice (DOJ) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump are tested by the Mideast, Helene and the port strike in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A trio of new trials — a devastating hurricane, expanding conflict in the Mideast and a dockworkers strike that threatens the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pennsylvania Republican in key swing-state Senate race backs using military to fight fentanyl
item.description: The Republican challenger trying to flip the U.S. Senate seat in swing-state Pennsylvania says he'll press for U.S. military action in Mexico to target fentanyl trafficking networks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: California firefighters in Hurricane Helene relief team recovering after crash in Texas
item.description: In a statement on Facebook, the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department thanked the community for their support during this difficult time.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: A US bomb from World War II explodes at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — An unexploded U.S. bomb from World War II that had been buried at a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, causing a large crater in a taxiway and the cancellation of more than 80 flights but no injuries, Japanese officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris and Biden are fanning out across the Southeast as devastation from Helene grows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the past four years, President Joe Biden has jetted off to survey damage and console victims after tornadoes, wildfires and tropical storms. It's not a role that Kamala Harris has played as vice president.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Are LGBTQ Jews welcome in Orthodox communities? This is how they are building spaces of their own
item.description: It was a heart-wrenching choice. But when Daniel Gammerman decided to never set foot back in an Orthodox synagogue, he thought of it as an act of love. Not toward the Jewish community he was born into, but to himself.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rescue mission delivering safe water system to remote areas hit by Hurricane Helene
item.description: A nonprofit organization that delivers clean water to communities hit by natural disasters is in North Carolina helping residents obtain clean drinking water after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dad treks miles through Hurricane Helene aftermath to walk daughter down aisle on her wedding day
item.description: A father's love knew no boundaries over the weekend as he walked nearly 30 miles through flood debris in South Carolina to Tennessee to walk his daughter down the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Helene: North Carolina residents fight for their survival as basic goods become scarce
item.description: Those affected by Hurricane Helene in the Southeast U.S. are now nearly five days without necessities, including water, gas, power and cellphone service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz, Vance double down on dueling abortion views
item.description: November 2024 will mark the first U.S. presidential election since the Supreme Court overturned its decision in Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Long water and power outages from Helene test patience in the Carolinas and Georgia
item.description: Many residents of the Carolinas still lack running water, cellphone service and electricity as rescuers continue their search for anyone still unaccounted for after Hurricane Helene's remnants caused flooding far inland.
item.dc:creator: Erik Verduzco, Jeff Amy And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump is returning this weekend to the town where an assassin's bullet grazed his ear in July.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz misleadingly claims to have been in Hong Kong during period tied to Tiananmen Square massacre
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Multiple news reports indicate that Democratic vice presidential nominee Minnesota Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 18-year-old charged in shooting death of dad in Adrian, Missouri
item.description: According to arrest reports, Carter J. Curtis faces one count of first-degree murder and one count of armed criminal action for allegedly shooting his father with a rifle.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: When do hurricane names get retired?
item.description: With nearly two months to go until the end of what was predicted to be a "hyperactive" hurricane season, many residents along Florida's Gulf Coast and southeastern states are grappling with the damage and destruction left in the wake of the latest hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Helene's death toll rises to more than 130 as supplies are rushed to areas in need
item.description: A crisis unfolded in Asheville, North Carolina, as officials pledged to get more water, food and other supplies to flood-stricken areas without power and cellular service Monday, days after Hurricane Helene ripped across the U.S. Southeast. The death toll from the storm surpassed 130.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department will launch civil rights review into 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — The Justice Department announced Monday it plans to launch a review of the 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre, an attack by a white mob on a thriving Black district that is considered one of the worst single acts of violence against Black people in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI to pay $22M to settle claims of sexual discrimination at training academy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI agreed Monday to pay more than $22 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging female recruits were singled out for dismissal in training and routinely harassed by instructors with sexually charged comments about their breast size, false allegations of infidelity and
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Rick Scott calls for Senate to reconvene to consider Helene disaster aid
item.description: Sen. Rick Scott (R-Fla.) on Monday called for the Senate to “immediately reconvene” once damage assessments are completed so lawmakers can consider an emergency aid package in response to the devastation left by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WGN 9

item.title: Maya Rudolph as Harris and Dana Carvey as Biden open the 50th season of 'Saturday Night Live'
item.description: “Saturday Night Live” began its 50th season with a parade of former co-stars, including Maya Rudolph as Vice President Kamala Harris, Andy Samberg as her husband Doug Emhoff and Dana Carvey as President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kris Kristofferson, singer-songwriter and actor, dies at 88
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kris Kristofferson, a Rhodes scholar with a deft writing style and rough charisma who became a country music superstar and an A-list Hollywood actor, has died. Kristofferson died at his home on Maui, Hawaii, on Saturday, family spokeswoman Ebie McFarland said in an email.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing themselves for a messy aftermath to Election Day as polls show the race between former President Trump and Vice President Harris is so close in several battleground states that it could take days to determine the winner.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Supplies arrive by plane and mule as Helene death toll passes 100
item.description: Desperate residents isolated by washed-out roads and the lack of power and cellular service in western North Carolina lined up Monday for fresh water and a chance to message loved ones that they were alive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kris Kristofferson, country music superstar and actor, dies at 88
item.description: Kris Kristofferson, a Rhodes scholar with a deft writing style and rough charisma who became a country music superstar and A-list Hollywood actor, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This Halloween decoration can be dangerous for deer, wildlife officials warn
item.description: Fall is in full swing and many have already decked out their properties with Halloween decorations, but wildlife experts say that one decoration in particular can be harmful to deer in the area.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, Fox 5

item.title: John Ashton of 'Beverly Hills Cop' dies at 76
item.description: In a career that spanned more than 50 years, Ashton was a regular face across TV series and films, including "Midnight Run,” “Little Big League” and “Gone Baby Gone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tropical Weather Latest: US Southeast struggles to recover in Hurricane Helene's aftermath
item.description: Massive rains brought by Hurricane Helene have left many people stranded or homeless as the cleanup begins from the monster tempest that killed at least 84 people. Helene has caused billions of dollars in destruction across a wide swath of the U.S. Southeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man killed, sister pistol-whipped while delivering pizza in Illinois
item.description: An Illinois family is pleading for answers after a young man was killed and his sister pistol whipped while out together on a pizza delivery.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4
